pageTimings.text: Spark had a cold. Usually a cold was nothing much. But Spark's magic crackled around him all the time. A cold, for Spark, was a very big problem.
He sneezed at breakfast. The porridge turned into a bird. The bird flew out of the window. Spark stared at his empty bowl.
Don't sneeze again, said Wren. I will try not to, said Spark. His nose twitched.
He sneezed in the Whispering Meadow. Every flower changed colour at the same moment. The meadow turned entirely blue. It was very quiet.
Sorry, said Spark. The flowers looked at him. He looked at them. His nose twitched again.
Spark tried to hold the sneeze in. His eyes went watery. His magic began to build and build. The sparkles around him grew very bright.
Whatever you do, said Fern, looking at the Crystal Stream, do NOT sneeze near the water.
Spark sneezed near the Crystal Stream.
Every fish in the stream turned purple. The water sang in a different key. One fish turned into a frog for a moment, and then turned back.
Sorry, said Spark. This was his third sorry. He meant all three of them very much.
I think, said Dewdrop slowly, we should find you a cold remedy before anything else changes.
Wren found a cold remedy in her spell book. She read it very, very carefully. Forwards? said Spark. Forwards, said Wren.
The remedy worked. Spark's cold was gone. The flowers slowly turned back to their usual colours. The fish returned to normal.
Spark breathed in carefully. I think, he said, I might need to sneeze again. Everyone took a large step back.
